authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(139) — Slinkard Creek and tributaries
sources.quotedText: Slinkard Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.) upstream from a department of Fish and Wildlife rock gabbion barrier (38.606976°N, 119.567687°W). The barrier is located approximately 5-6 miles upstream from the Hwy 89 and Hwy 395 junction.

sources.title: Slinkard Creek Restoration Key To Preserving Native Lahontan Cutthroat Trout | California Trout
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 234712, "Slinkard Creek", Stream, Mono Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":234712,"gaz_name":"Slinkard Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"}; geometry multipoint with mouth/source endpoints converting to approx (-119.527403, 38.637695) and (-119.554263, 38.533165) — the file's recorded coordinate (-119.567687, 38.606976) falls geographically between these two endpoints along the creek's course and is not itself a GNIS point; it is instead taken directly from the CCR §7.50(b)(139) regulatory text, which cites the CDFW rock-gabbion barrier's coordinates as "38.606976°N, 119.567687°W" verbatim. Confirmed consistent with the creek's GNIS-mapped course; coordinate retained as-is since it identifies the specific regulated barrier point rather than the creek's generic mouth/source.

groups.humanSummary: Slinkard Creek and tributaries above a CDFW rock gabbion barrier: year-round catch-and-release, artificial flies with barbless hooks only, as part of Lahontan cutthroat trout restoration.
groups.verbatimText: Slinkard Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.) upstream from a department of Fish and Wildlife rock gabbion barrier (38.606976°N, 119.567687°W). The barrier is located approximately 5-6 miles upstream from the Hwy 89 and Hwy 395 junction. All year. Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Special regulations apply only to Slinkard Creek and its tributaries upstream of a CDFW rock gabbion barrier at approximately 38.606976°N, 119.567687°W, about 5-6 miles upstream from the Hwy 89/Hwy 395 junction. Water downstream of the barrier is not covered by this listing.

regulations.humanSummary: Special regulations apply upstream of the CDFW rock gabbion barrier at 38.606976°N, 119.567687°W
regulations.verbatimText: Slinkard Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.) upstream from a department of Fish and Wildlife rock gabbion barrier (38.606976°N, 119.567687°W). The barrier is located approximately 5-6 miles upstream from the Hwy 89 and Hwy 395 junction.

parameters.note: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used; no lures or bait.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: No trout may be kept from the regulated reach above the barrier; all trout caught must be released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
